findings.title: Qualification smoke flows still cite legacy reviewer-output refs
findings.rationale: The reviewed change intentionally makes record-review-outcome reject arbitrary reviewer-output:<lane> evidence and require real inbox refs shaped reviewer-output:<bundle-id>:<output-id>. However the live qualify-codex runtime and parity smoke flows still call record-review-outcome with legacy refs such as reviewer-output:qualification-runtime-spec-review, reviewer-output:qualification-runtime-post-completion-review, reviewer-output:qualification-runtime-mission-close-review, reviewer-output:parity-spec-review, and reviewer-output:parity-mission-close-review. Those refs cannot satisfy parse_reviewer_output_evidence_ref because they have no output id and do not correspond to persisted inbox artifacts, so the required qualification success test will report failed runtime/parity gates even though the focused runtime_internal proof rows pass.
findings.suggested_next_action: Update the qualification smoke flows to capture the child evidence snapshot, call record-reviewer-output, and pass the returned evidence_ref into every record-review-outcome payload; adjust the parity helper payloads to accept the real reviewer-output evidence refs instead of hard-coded lane strings, then rerun the qualification CLI test.
